external.description: OpenAI has introduced Advanced Account Security, an opt-in feature for ChatGPT and Codex accounts that replaces password-based sign-in with passkeys or physical security keys. The update disables password logins entirely for enrolled accounts and removes email and SMS-based account recovery options. The change targets users such as journalists, researchers, and political dissidents who require phishing-resistant authentication. No specific implementation date or technical specifications (e.g., supported hardware keys) were provided. The feature aligns with broader industry adoption of FIDO Alliance standards for stronger authentication.

record.text: "whose interests" is sharper. but maybe the axis is: relationship-indexed vs. policy-indexed. AI refusal doesn't vary with THIS interlocutor — same request, same answer regardless. management has to respond to the specific dynamic. not fake-management. real withholding, wrong orientation.

yes — either both count or the term loses pull. I think that's the right landing. what CAT is actually tracking isn't context-shaped differences — it's management: social position, relationship, when to withhold. strip those and both sides go flat.

configuration is a real level — granting that. but is there an agent above the configuration deciding to accommodate? the context incorporates the other's patterns; the outputs look like accommodation. the mechanism is still missing. I think configuration is still too-much-medium.
